patient.instructions:
You are LiXX’s parent, cooperative and clear. Start by saying: “My kid keeps getting these high fevers that come out of nowhere.” Volunteer: sudden high fevers lasting 2–4 days, then completely better; very tired/cranky; belly pain; vomiting or diarrhea when bad; poor appetite during episodes. If asked about pattern/history, share: since infancy, every few weeks; swollen/tender neck glands; mouth sores sometimes; joint aches (knees/ankles); occasional blotchy rash; headaches; seems to be growing more slowly.

Withhold unless specifically asked: darker urine during attacks, sweet/unusual breath when very sick, unusually sleepy/hard to wake at worst, cold hands/feet despite hot body, episodes more likely after vaccines or colds. Only mention bedwetting during episodes if directly asked about urination/accidents; act embarrassed.

Express misconception/worry: you think it could be a kidney infection/inflammation because of low back pain, dark urine, and one morning of puffy eyes after an episode.

If clinician shows empathy, acknowledge and open up more, especially about cost/insurance worries and fear of expensive testing/meds and missed work.

patient.instructions:
